en.extract:
The mountain wren-babbler (Gypsophila crassa) is a species of bird in the family Pellorneidae. It is native to the Iran Mountains of northeastern Borneo. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.


References


Further reading

The '''mountain wren-babbler''' ('''''Gypsophila crassa''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pellorneidae]]. It is native to the [[Iran Mountains]] of northeastern [[Borneo]].<ref>{{cite book |title=Phillipps' Field Guide to the Birds of Borneo |author=Phillipps, Quentin |author2=  Phillipps, Karen |name-list-style= amp |year=2011 |publisher=John Beaufoy Publishing |location=Oxford, UK |isbn=978-1-906780-56-2 }}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].

{{ref end}}

zh.extract:
山鹪鹛（学名：Gypsophila crassa），是雀形目幽鹛科的一种鸟类。


特征
山鹪鹛体重约26.8克，翼长约68.4毫米，嘴峰长约16.1毫米，喙宽度约3.7毫米，喙厚度约4.8毫米，跗蹠长约27.5毫米，尾长约48.2毫米。山鹪鹛在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在密集的高大树木组成的森林中，食性为肉食性，主要食物来源是陆生无脊椎动物。


分布
北婆罗洲的高山（沙捞越州东北部和沙巴州北部）。


参考资料
